title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. lanceolata
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. lanceolata, also known as the bellflower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It is a perennial herb with blue, bell-shaped flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as a natural remedy for digestive problems. Campanula rotundifolia subsp. lanceolata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
